sections.paragraph:
παρακαταθήκη, ης, ἡ (παρακατατίθημι; Hdt. et al. This is the real Attic form [s. παραθήκη], but is also found in Aristot., EN 5, 8, 5, 1135b, 4; Polyb. 5, 74, 5; Diod S 4, 58, 6; 15, 76, 1; Plut., Anton. 924 [21, 4]; Aelian, VH 4, 1; Vett. Val. p. 60, 21; ApcEsdr 6:3 p. 31, 7 Tdf. al. [in sense of ‘soul’]; Philo, Spec. Leg. 4, 30–38; Jos., Bell. 3, 372, Ant. 4, 285; ins, pap, LXX) someth. given for safekeeping, deposit 1 Ti 6:20; 2 Ti 1:14, both v.l. (for παραθήκην. Used w. φυλάσσω as Socrat., Ep. 28, 6. S. Mishnah: Baba Metzia 3; cp. Ps.-Phocyl. 13 παρθεσίην τήρειν, πίστιν δʼ ἐν πᾶσι φυλάσσειν ‘keep watch over a deposit, and in everything observe fidelity’; other reff. PvanderHorst, The Sentences of Phocylides ’78, 120–21); Hm 3:2.—S. DELG s.v. τίθημι. M-M. TW. Sv.

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

someth. someth. = something

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Mishnah Mishnah = HDanby, The Mishnah—Lists 5, 6

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Hm Hm = Mandates

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 765
